findings.evidence: `rg` hit: lines 73-75 define `ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f (Ici 0) ∧ ... IsCompletelyMonotone (derivWithin f (Ici 0))`; roadmap lines 175-176 call for the standard Bernstein-function relationships and Lévy-Khinchine representation, which include Bernstein functions with singular derivative at 0.
findings.fix: Define the standard predicate with continuity/nonnegativity on `[0,∞)`, smoothness on `(0,∞)`, and complete monotonicity of the ordinary derivative on `(0,∞)`; keep this stronger version only under a name that advertises the closed-smooth restriction, or first add the missing open-half-line completely-monotone prerequisite.
findings.issue: `IsBernsteinFunction` requires `ContDiffOn ℝ ∞ f (Ici 0)` and `IsCompletelyMonotone (derivWithin f (Ici 0))`, so it excludes standard Bernstein functions whose derivative is completely monotone only on `(0,∞)` and may blow up at 0, e.g. `t ↦ sqrt t`.

summary: The predicate is named and documented as Bernstein functions, but it formalizes only a closed-smooth subclass with finite right derivatives of all orders at 0. That is too strong for the standard object needed by the roadmap's Bernstein/Lévy-Khinchine development.
